channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇼 Rwandan genocide suspect Lucien Kabuga dies at 93 in UN custody
item.description: Lucien Kabuga, accused of financing the 1994 genocide in Rwanda, has died in custody at age 93. He was one of the last remaining suspects from the international tribunal. | Africa - Reuters

item.title: 🚀 BlackRock weighs $5-10B investment in SpaceX IPO
item.description: BlackRock is in discussions to invest billions in SpaceX upcoming initial public offering. The investment would be one of the largest pre-IPO commitments in recent market history. | Technology - Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Prime Minister Starmer set to approve $24B defense spending increase
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound increase in defense spending as he faces political pressure. The Times reports the increase comes amid global security concerns. | Defense Industry - Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇭 Eight killed as freight train hits bus in Thailand
item.description: At least eight people died and 32 were injured when a freight train struck a bus at a rail crossing in Bangkok, igniting a fire that engulfed the vehicle. | Asia-Pacific - Reuters

item.title: 🇸🇱 Sierra Leone agrees to accept hundreds of West Africans deported by US
item.description: Sierra Leone has struck a deal with the Trump administration to accept hundreds of deported West African migrants, the latest in a series of agreements aimed at accelerating US removals. | Africa - Reuters

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇦 France Ready to Develop Ballistic Missile Defense With Ukraine, Zelensky Says
item.description: French President discussed strengthening capabilities to repel Russian attacks, with Zelensky calling the cooperation an important step in air defense development. | Defense Industry - Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russians Worry More About Strikes at Home Than Front Line for First Time Since 2022
item.description: A new poll shows shifting public anxiety inside Russia, with growing concern about domestic attacks versus battlefield risks as Ukrainian drone strikes intensify. | Ukraine-Russia War - Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱🇮🇷 US-Israel Preparing for Possible Renewal of Strikes on Iran, Report Says
item.description: Haaretz reports preparations underway for potential new strikes against Iranian targets as diplomatic negotiations remain stalled. | US-Iran War - Haaretz

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Captures Two Villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv Region
item.description: Russian forces have taken control of Borova and Kutkivka settlements in Kharkiv, marking continued territorial gains as Moscow presses its offensive in eastern Ukraine. | Ukraine-Russia War - Reuters

item.title: ⚠️ Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO from Belarus
item.description: Ukraine's president says Moscow is seeking to draw Belarus deeper into the war and weighing plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory. | Ukraine-Russia War - Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Nations
item.description: Prime Minister Carney strengthens alliances with "middle powers" as the US becomes a less reliable partner, focusing on Arctic security amid growing geopolitical tensions. | Arctic - Reuters

item.title: ⚖️ Consumers Sue Amazon Over Unlawful Trump Tariff Costs
item.description: Amazon faces lawsuit from consumers seeking refunds for higher prices resulting from tariffs the Supreme Court later ruled were unlawfully imposed by President Trump. | Economy - Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US CEOs Pursue Diplomacy in Beijing Following Trump Summit
item.description: American business leaders from Citigroup and Goldman Sachs meet Chinese officials, hoping political goodwill will translate into regulatory approvals and lucrative deals. | US-China - Reuters

item.title: 🇰🇲 Comoros fuel protests turn deadly amid Iran war price spikes
item.description: One person killed and five injured in clashes on the Comorian island of Anjouan as unrest over fuel price hikes spreads across the archipelago, linked to global supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz crisis. | Africa - Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvia faces political uncertainty as president names opposition lawmaker as PM candidate
item.description: Latvian President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister after Evika Silina resigned, amid regional security concerns. | Europe - Reuters

item.title: ⛽ US and Brazil ethanol exports surge as countries seek fuel alternatives
item.description: The world's two largest ethanol producers are seeing export increases as consuming countries seek alternative fuel sources while the Strait of Hormuz crisis continues. | Energy - Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳 China criticizes US Hormuz resolution at UN as timing and content questioned
item.description: China's UN ambassador criticized a proposed US-Bahraini resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, saying the content and timing were inappropriate and passing it would not be helpful. | Diplomacy - Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇹🇪🇺 Italy's Meloni wins European backing for third-country migrant return hubs
item.description: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni hailed a shift in European migration policy as her controversial approach to offshore migrant processing gains support across the Council of Europe. | Europe - Anadolu Agency

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE States All Actions Were Defensive Measures to Protect Sovereignty
item.description: The United Arab Emirates foreign ministry issued a statement saying all actions taken by the Gulf country were defensive measures aimed at protecting its sovereignty, civilians, and vital infrastructure amid regional tensions. | Middle East - Reuters

item.title: ⚽🇮🇷 FIFA Officials to Meet Iranian FA Over World Cup Participation
item.description: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom will meet Iranian Football Federation officials in Istanbul to offer reassurance over Iran participation in the upcoming World Cup amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict. | Diplomacy - Reuters

item.title: 🎤🏳️ Eurovision Song Contest Final Proceeds Amid Gaza Boycott
item.description: Five countries have withdrawn from the Eurovision Song Contest final over Israel participation, with the event taking place against the backdrop of ongoing Middle East tensions. | Europe - Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇺🇺🇸 US Reportedly Preparing to Indict Former Cuban Leader Raul Castro
item.description: The US Justice Department is reportedly preparing to criminally indict former Cuban leader Raul Castro in the coming days, marking a significant development in US-Cuba relations. | Latin America - AP

item.title: 🦠 Congo Confirms New Ebola Outbreak with 80 Deaths
item.description: Africa CDC confirms Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo's Ituri province. Health officials are mobilizing response efforts to contain the spread. | Africa - Reuters

item.title: 💧 US Proposes Dramatic Colorado River Water Cuts Amid Drought
item.description: US government plans could cut up to 40% of water supplies to Arizona, California and Nevada. The drought-stricken Colorado River basin faces unprecedented pressure from climate change and overuse. | US Politics - Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump-Targeted Senator Cassidy Faces Louisiana Primary Test
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, targeted by Trump for retribution over impeachment vote, faces competitive primary in Louisiana. Results will test Trump's influence on GOP congressional races. | US Politics - Reuters
